_comment: Fresh, specific volunteer opportunities — NOT a evergreen list. Each entry was verified directly on the organization's own volunteer page (never United Way, Idealist, or Front Porch Forum — see /Users/stephendavis/Desktop/newsletter/reference/research/06-volunteer-listings.md for why). blurb text is an original one-line summary, not copied from the org's site. Every entry must keep last_checked current: js/volunteer.js hides any entry whose last_checked is more than 14 days old (MAX_FRESH_AGE_DAYS), so a stale record just disappears instead of showing an out-of-date 'opportunity'.
review_cadence: Re-verify each entry against the org's own page before it turns 14 days old, then bump last_checked to the day it was re-confirmed. If a listing is no longer current, remove it rather than leaving a stale date. This is a manual pull, not an automated feed — no United Way Volunteer Connection data here (that partnership request is still pending Megan Bridges' reply).

opportunities.title: Gleaning & Food Access
opportunities.blurb: Pack and distribute free produce for the Fair Share CSA pickup — sign up for a specific shift.
opportunities.date: Weekly Wed & Thu through July, then Mondays July–October 2026

opportunities.title: Conservation Nursery
opportunities.blurb: Tend native saplings and trees in the greenhouse and fields while the nursery's field crew is out planting.

opportunities.title: Suds & Sailboats
opportunities.blurb: Clean and prep the CSC's sailboat fleet, then cap the evening with a beer at Foam Brewers — no sailing experience needed.
opportunities.date: Third Thursday of the month through Oct 31, 2026 (next: July 23, 5–7pm)

opportunities.title: Morning Cat & Small Animal Care
opportunities.blurb: Feed and clean enclosures for cats and small animals who aren't yet up for adoption.

opportunities.title: Group Volunteer Day
opportunities.blurb: Bring a company, school, or community group of up to 15 to volunteer together at the shelter.
opportunities.date: Book a 3–4 hour group day, by application

opportunities.title: Activity Facilitator
opportunities.blurb: Plan and lead a short activity — crafts, games, social-emotional learning — for families living at the shelter.
opportunities.date: Weekdays 4–8pm (flexible) or weekends, up to 2-hour sessions
opportunities.location: COTS Family Shelter, 278 Main St, Burlington, VT

opportunities.title: Respite Provider
opportunities.blurb: Give a shelter family a short break by watching their kids on-site, with COTS staff present. Background check required.
opportunities.location: COTS Family Shelter, 278 Main St, Burlington, VT
